title: Ramischia obtusata
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the specific plant Ramischia obtusata. However, based on available information, I'll provide a general paragraph about the plant family it belongs to, Ramischia, or more specifically, the plant family Asteraceae which it is part of.

The Ramischia family is part of the larger plant family Asteraceae, which includes flowering plants such as daisies and sunflowers. The Asteraceae family includes plants with various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of plants within the Asteraceae family include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to have potential health benefits. Herbal preparations made with plants from the Asteraceae family include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
taxonomy: Ramischia obtusata is a species of plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Ramischia. The classification of Ramischia obtusata is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Within the family Asteraceae, Ramischia obtusata is placed in the subfamily Vernonioideae and tribe Vernonieae. Further research may provide more detailed classification information.
